ternary operations overlap, the first replaces every active summand on its
support by scalars, so the second composition is zero in either order.  If
their supports are disjoint, the two odd operations anticommute.  Hence the
square of their sum is zero.  QED.

For `(q,r,s)=(3,1,2)`, the canonical allocation is `(1,0,2)`.  On the word
of Lemma 23A2 it maps

```text
Pi(a^3V_3) tensor aV_1 tensor a^2V_2
 -> a^2 1 tensor 1 tensor a^4 1.
```

The choices of one of the two minus factors and one of the two plus-two
factors give four distinct outputs, so this ternary differential pairs all
four offending odd invariants with even ones.

The remaining obstruction is unbounded arity rather than the
Maurer--Cartan equation.  For example, in

```text
R=[5^-,3^-,1^+,2^+,2^+,2^+,2^+],          p=7,
```

the outer grade is `a^(17-7)=a^10` and contains the odd invariant

```text
a^5V_5 tensor aV_1 tensor a^2V_2 tensor a^2V_2,
```

because `V_1 tensor V_2 tensor V_2` contains `V_5`.  No pair among
`V_1,V_2,V_2` contains `V_5`, so every ternary evaluation vanishes on this
class; an arity-four evaluation is necessary.

More generally, take `q=2m+1` and

```text
R_m=[q^-,3^-,1^+,(2^+)^(2m)],              p=q+2.
```

The outer grade contains a class using `V_q`, `V_1`, and `m` of the
`V_2` factors.  Their highest weights sum to `q`, while every proper
subcollection has highest weight below `q`.  Thus killing this class on the
minimal effective object requires an operation involving the odd factor and
all `m+1` active plus factors.  The necessary arity is unbounded.  This
leads to an unbounded-hyperedge rank problem, even though the pure
evaluation operations themselves satisfy square zero automatically.

For exactly two minus factors, that rank problem has an exact bipartite
form.  Fix an outer `a`-degree.  A left vertex consists of

```text
(i,A,lambda, scalar grades off A),                    (P23.11k)
```

where minus factor `i` contributes its odd `a^(q_i)V_(q_i)` summand, `A`
is the indexed set of plus factors in their nontrivial `a^(r_j)V_(r_j)`
summands, `lambda` is a fusion-path basis vector in

```text
Inv(V_(q_i) tensor tensor_(j in A)V_(r_j)),
```

and every untouched factor has a specified scalar grade.  A right vertex
is an all-scalar allocation of the same total degree.  Join a left vertex
to a right vertex when the untouched scalar grades agree and the active
coordinates can be assigned

```text
0<=t_i<=q_i,              0<=t_j<=r_j,
sum_(i union A)t=(q_i+sum_(j in A)r_j)/2.             (P23.11l)
```

The edge map is the dual invariant `lambda^*` followed by insertion into
those scalar summands.

**Lemma 23A6 (pure-evaluation Hall reduction).**  A pure-evaluation
differential can be chosen injective on the outer odd subspace with exactly
one essential minus summand if and only if the bipartite graph
`(P23.11k)--(P23.11l)` satisfies

```text
|X|<=|N(X)|                         for every left set X. (P23.11m)
```

For every fixed active support `i union A`, these inequalities hold
automatically.

**Proof.**  The independent dual path functionals make the allowed
differential entries arbitrary scalars on precisely the displayed
bipartite support.  Full column rank is therefore equivalent to a matching,
which is `(P23.11m)`.  For the fixed-support assertion, order the active
labels `d_1,...,d_n` and write a fusion path as

```text
h_0=0, h_1,...,h_n=0.
```

Set

```text
alpha_k=(d_k+h_k-h_(k-1))/2.                          (P23.11n)
```

Fusion admissibility gives `0<=alpha_k<=d_k`, and telescoping gives
`sum_k alpha_k=sum_k d_k/2`.  Thus `(alpha_k)` is a neighboring scalar
allocation.  Conversely,
`h_k=h_(k-1)+2alpha_k-d_k`, so this map is injective on fusion paths.
QED.

Consequently the remaining theorem is not path capacity inside one support;
it is simultaneous assignment of scalar-grade markers across different
active supports and pre-existing scalar allocations.  The Hall condition is
a structured strengthening of aggregate dimension positivity, so it is not
equivalent to `(PCP)` merely from total dimensions.

There is a simultaneous injection for arbitrary top-path supports.  Fix the
selected minus factor and suppose

```text
q=sum_(j in A)r_j.
```

The invariant on that active support is the unique top fusion path.  Assign

```text
t_q=q-|A|,       t_j=1 for j in A,       t_j=0 off A. (P23.11o)
```

This is admissible because every `r_j>=1`; its index sum is `q`, half the
active label sum.  The coordinates equal to one recover `A`, so
`(P23.11o)` is collision-free across any family of top supports.

The family `R_m` above illustrates that unbounded arity need not cause a
rank failure.  For every `m`-subset `A` of the `2m` indexed plus-two
positions, the top fusion path

```text
V_(2m+1) subset V_1 tensor tensor_(j in A)V_2
```

is unique.  Send it to the scalar allocation having index `m+1` on the
selected minus factor, index zero on the plus-one factor, index one on
each selected plus-two factor, and index zero elsewhere.  The index sum is
`(m+1)+m=2m+1`, exactly half the active label sum.  The positions carrying
index one recover `A`, and the positive minus index records which equal
minus factor was selected when `m=1`.  Hence these
`binom(2m,m)` outer odd classes have a collision-free matching for every
`m`.

The universal scalar-output Hall theorem is nevertheless false.

**False Target 23A6A (cross-support scalar-marker theorem).**  Consider the
remainder

```text
R=[3^-,1^+,5^+,(2^+)^5]
```

at outer target `p=7`.  Its relevant `a`-degree is
`19-7=12`, or scalar-index sum six.  Fix the plus-five factor in scalar
grade zero.  Let `X` contain every state whose active support consists of
the plus-one factor and a `k`-subset `B` of the five indexed plus-two
factors, for `1<=k<=4`; give the inactive plus-two factors scalar indices
summing to `4-k`, and include every fusion path.

The four fusion multiplicities are

```text
dim Inv(V_3 tensor V_1 tensor V_2^k)=1,2,5,12,
                                             k=1,2,3,4,
```

while the numbers of inactive scalar assignments are respectively

```text
[x^(4-k)](1+x+x^2)^(5-k)=16,6,2,1.
```

Therefore

```text
|X|=5*1*16+10*2*6+10*5*2+5*12*1=360.               (P23.11p)
```

On the right, the scalar caps are `(3,1,2,2,2,2,2)` and their indices sum
to six, giving

```text
[x^6](1+x+x^2+x^3)(1+x)(1+x+x^2)^5=312
```

ambient allocations.  Write `C` for the inactive subset of plus-two
positions.  The edge condition is

```text
sum_(j in C)t_j=|C|-1,
```

or equivalently `#zeros in C=#twos in C+1`.  A nonempty proper `C` exists
exactly when at least one of the five indices is zero, by taking a singleton
zero.  Hence the only excluded allocations have no zero: five permutations
of `(2,1,1,1,1)` with the minus and plus-one indices both zero, and the two
allocations with all five indices one and those two remaining indices
`(1,0)` or `(0,1)`.  Thus

```text
|N(X)|=312-7=305<360=|X|.                            (P23.11q)
```

This exact Hall deficit of `55` proves that no scalar-marker choice,
including `(P23.11n)`, complements, rotations, or zero-sum transfers, can
give the universal injection.

The strict exact C++ verifier
`character_ring_iter/verify_su2_fdc_evaluation_hall.cpp` recomputes the
four fusion multiplicities, both bounded-composition counts, and the full
neighbor enumeration.  Its replay is recorded in
`certificates/su2_fdc_evaluation_hall.log`.

At the level of the reduced essential-source block, retaining the first
non-scalar carrier appears to repair this failure.  For `q=3`, the
essential odd class is `a^3V_3=H_1(j=0)` and the carrier is
`a^4V_2=H_2(j=0)`.  On a support containing the plus-one factor and `k`
plus-two factors, use

```text
pi_2:V_3 tensor V_1 -> V_2
```

and retain the active `V_2^k` tensor.  Since

```text
V_3 tensor V_1=V_2 direct-sum V_4,
```

the original, carrier, and residual multiplicities for `k=1,2,3,4` are

```text
m_k=(1,2,5,12),       c_k=(1,1,3,6),       d_k=(0,1,2,6). (P23.11r)
```

The carrier outputs remember the indexed active support and inactive scalar
grades, so they are collision-free.  Their total rank is

```text
5*16*1+10*6*1+10*2*3+5*1*6=230.                    (P23.11s)
```

The residual `V_4` blocks have `130` directions.  Their exact bipartite
graph against the `312` scalar allocations has matching rank `130`.
Consequently

```text
230+130=360,                                          (P23.11t)
```

for that reduced block.  This count is not the rank of a differential on
the full `H_1/H_2` space, because `H_2(j)` is already paired with the odd
column `H_1(j+1)`.

**Lemma 23A6B (carrier Schur-complement criterion).**  Let `C` be a paired
odd `H_1` block, `C'` its isomorphic even `H_2` block, `E` the essential
odd block, and `S` the remaining even block.  After normalizing the
pairing `C -> C'` to the identity, any proposed differential has block
matrix

```text
             C   E
partial = [  I   A  ] : C direct-sum E -> C' direct-sum S.
          [  D   B  ]
```

Its rank is

```text
rank(partial)=dim(C)+rank(B-DA).                      (P23.11t1)
```

In particular, if the paired columns have no redistribution map `D`, the
carrier block `A:E -> C'` contributes no rank beyond the identity pairing.

**Proof.**  Subtract from each column of `E` the corresponding linear
combination, prescribed by `A`, of the columns of `C`.  This invertible
column operation changes the matrix to

```text
[ I    0   ]
[ D  B-DA ],
```

which proves `(P23.11t1)`.  QED.

Therefore `(P23.11s)--(P23.11t)` prove only that the essential block embeds
after the paired `H_1` columns have been suppressed.  They do not repair
the full Hall deficit.  The same strict verifier and certificate recompute
the reduced carrier and residual ranks; they do not construct `D` or check
the Schur complement.  A valid carrier proof must redistribute the paired
odd columns and prove full rank of `B-DA`.

There is a uniform carrier hierarchy for every fixed active support in
ordinary `SU(2)`.  The carrier types in `(P23.11a)` can be rewritten as

```text
H_2(j)=a^(q+1+j)V_(q-1-j)
      =a^(2q-h)V_h,             0<=h<q.              (P23.11u)
```

For a chosen plus label `r`, every channel `h in q star r` with `h<q`
has the unique degree-preserving map

```text
a^qV_q tensor a^rV_r
 -> a^(2q-h)V_h tensor a^(2t)1,
t=(r-q+h)/2.                                         (P23.11v)
```

Fusion admissibility makes `t` an integer in `[0,r]`; these are exactly all
pairwise maps into the retained `H_2` carriers.

**Lemma 23A7 (first-descent carrier injection).**  Fix an indexed active
support, order its plus labels `r_1,...,r_n`, and choose a fusion-path basis

```text
q=h_0 -> h_1 -> ... -> h_n=0.
```

Let `t` be the first index with `h_t<q`, and put

```text
alpha_i=(r_i+h_i-h_(i-1))/2,             1<=i<=t.     (P23.11w)
```

Project the consumed prefix to `V_(h_t)`, place it in the carrier
`a^(2q-h_t)V_(h_t)`, send each prefix plus factor to scalar grade
`alpha_i`, and leave the active suffix and its fusion path unchanged.  This
is a degree-preserving injection on every fixed-support odd path space.

**Proof.**  Fusion admissibility gives
`0<=alpha_i<=r_i`.  Telescoping gives

```text
sum_(i<=t)alpha_i
 =(sum_(i<=t)r_i+h_t-q)/2,
```

so the output degree is

```text
(2q-h_t)+2sum_(i<=t)alpha_i
 =q+sum_(i<=t)r_i,
```

equal to the consumed input degree.  The carrier type and surviving suffix
identify the first descent.  The prefix path is recovered recursively from
`h_i=h_(i-1)+2alpha_i-r_i`, and the retained suffix path recovers the rest.
Every invariant path has a first descent because `h_n=0<q`.  QED.

Lemma 23A7 concerns only the map from the essential `H_1(0)` block to
carrier outputs.  It does not assert injectivity of a differential on the
full odd space: by Lemma 23A6B, the paired `H_1(j+1)` columns must also be
redistributed before their `H_2(j)` rows provide net capacity.

First descent does not by itself retain enough information across different
supports, even if its factor order is allowed to depend on the source.

**Lemma 23A8A (Johnson-graph carrier collision).**  Fix `q>=2` and take
`n>=2q` indexed plus-one factors.  For every `q`-subset `A` of their
positions, use the odd class `a^qV_q`, make exactly the positions in `A`
active, and put every inactive position in scalar grade zero.  No choice of
source-dependent orders makes the first-descent maps of Lemma 23A7
injective on this family.

**Proof.**  Each source has the unique invariant path

```text
q -> q-1 -> ... -> 1 -> 0.
```

The first active factor is therefore consumed at scalar index

```text
(1+(q-1)-q)/2=0,
```

and produces the fixed carrier `a^(q+1)V_(q-1)`.  The output retains only
the other `q-1` active positions and their unique descending suffix path;
all remaining positions have scalar index zero.  Thus a source `A` can
reach only the targets indexed by its `(q-1)`-subsets.  For the full left
family the neighborhood has size `binom(n,q-1)`, whereas

```text
binom(n,q)/binom(n,q-1)=(n-q+1)/q>1
```

when `n>=2q`.  Hall's inequality fails.  The smallest case is `q=2,n=4`,
with six sources and four carrier targets.  QED.

This is an obstruction only to carrier-only support encoding.  The same
family has a collision-free pure evaluation: put scalar index one on every
active plus position and index zero on the minus and inactive positions.
The scalar-index sum is `q`, exactly half the active label sum, and the
positions carrying one recover the source support.  Any uniform
construction must therefore choose between scalar markers and retained
carriers rather than expect either family alone to be injective.

For the reduced essential-source map, Lemma 23A7 removes every
within-support path Hall inequality.  Its remaining graph is purely
cross-support: collisions occur only when a factor consumed to scalar grade
`alpha_i` in one support is inactive with the same scalar grade in another.
This is a useful reduced graph, not the graph of the full differential;
Lemma 23A6B adds the independent Schur-complement obligation for the paired
odd columns.

The first fixed-order collision is already visible at three indexed
plus-one factors.  Take `q=2`, scalar-index grade two, and the two sources

```text
A_02: support {0,2}, path 2 -> 1 -> 0, t_1=0,
A_12: support {1,2}, path 2 -> 1 -> 0, t_0=0.        (P23.11x)
```

In the fixed indexed order, both first descents consume the earlier active
factor with scalar grade zero and retain factor two.  Their common target is

```text
a^3 V_1 tensor (a^0 1)_0 tensor (a^0 1)_1
          tensor (a^1 V_1)_2,                         (P23.11y)
```

with the two `V_1` factors coupled to `V_0`.  Thus the two-source
neighborhood has size one.  Starting each support cyclically at factor two
adds a different surviving-factor target to each source and repairs this
particular deficit.

Allowing every ordering does not give a universal repair.  Define the
permutation-saturated `H_2` prefix graph by allowing, for every active
support, every permutation of its indexed factors and every stopping prefix
whose current height is `h<q`; send that prefix to the carrier
`a^(2q-h)V_h`, with scalar grades given by `(P23.11w)`, and retain the
suffix.  Identifying suffix bases after arbitrary recoupling only enlarges
this graph.

**Proposition 23A8B (full `H_2` prefix-carrier Hall obstruction).**  For
`q=2` and four indexed plus-one factors at scalar-index grade two, the
permutation-saturated graph above has Hall deficit one.

**Proof.**  For each pair `0<=i<j<=3`, let `A_ij` have active support
`{i,j}`, put every inactive factor in scalar grade zero, and take the unique
path

```text
2 -> 1 -> 0.
```

There are six such sources.  If the map stops after one active factor, its
carrier is `a^3V_1`, the consumed scalar grade is zero, and its target is
determined only by the surviving index.  This gives four targets `R_j`,
one for each possible survivor.  If it stops after both active factors,
the carrier is `a^4V_0`, both scalar grades are zero, and every pair has the
same target `R_empty`.  These exhaust all permutations and all `H_2`
stopping prefixes because every support has two elements and its fusion
path is unique.  Hence

```text
|X|=binom(4,2)=6,       |N(X)|=4+1=5,       deficit=1. (P23.11z)
```

For the first-descent-only rule `R_empty` is absent, so the deficit is two.
Cyclic order and all permutations coincide on a two-element support.  QED.

Therefore the complete factorwise `H_2` prefix-carrier hierarchy, even
after permutation and recoupling saturation, cannot furnish the universal
cross-support injection.  This does not rule out a differential with
additional even carriers or genuinely non-prefix multi-factor operations.
The strict threaded C++ search
`character_ring_iter/search_su2_fdc_h2_recoupling_hall.cpp` verifies the
three-factor repair, recomputes `(P23.11z)`, and searches ordered label
words in the declared finite range.  Its replay is recorded in
`certificates/su2_fdc_h2_recoupling_hall.log`; the finite search is
corroboration, not the proof of Proposition 23A8B.

The missing rank in the smallest obstruction is supplied by exactly one
scalar marker.

**Proposition 23A8C (minimal reduced-graph augmentation).**  In the
`q=2,n=4` family of Proposition 23A8B, the reduced graph from the six
essential sources to otherwise-unused prefix-carrier targets has matching
rank five.  Adjoining the pure scalar evaluation for any one source raises
this reduced matching rank to six; hence one scalar target is necessary and
sufficient for this reduced graph.

**Proof.**  The five carrier targets in `(P23.11z)` give the upper bound.
They have matching rank five, for example

```text
A_01 -> R_0,   A_12 -> R_1,   A_23 -> R_2,
A_03 -> R_3,   A_02 -> R_empty.
```

Now send `A_01` instead to the all-scalar allocation having index one at
positions zero and one and index zero at the minus and the other two plus
positions.  Its index sum is two, exactly half the active label sum.  The
remaining five sources have the carrier matching

```text
A_02 -> R_0,   A_03 -> R_3,   A_12 -> R_2,
A_13 -> R_1,   A_23 -> R_empty.                       (P23.11z1)
```

All six targets are distinct.  Proposition 23A8B shows that no repair with
zero scalar targets is possible.  Permuting the four positions proves the
same statement for any chosen source.  QED.

This is not a chain-level repair.  Five carrier rows used in
`(P23.11z1)` displace five paired odd columns.  After the common
`H_1/H_2` block is cancelled, Lemma 23A6B requires the entire six-dimensional
boundary source, not only one chosen column, to be paid in the scalar
Schur complement.

There is also an exact representation-theoretic reduction of the one-minus
injection.  Work first in ordinary `SU(2)_x` by `SU(2)_y`.  Let

```text
S=Sym^(q-1)(V_(1,x) direct-sum V_(1,y)),
K_s=Hom_(SU(2)_y)(V_(s,y),K),
```

where `K` is any finite-dimensional module for the product group; each
`K_s` is regarded as an `SU(2)_x` module.

**Lemma 23A9 (one-minus boundary reduction).**  Put

```text
A=Hom_(SU(2)_y)(1,S tensor K),
B=Hom_(SU(2)_y)(V_(1,y),S tensor K).
```

Then

```text
V_(1,x) tensor A-B=V_(q,x) tensor K_0-K_q.            (P23.11z2)
```

Consequently an `SU(2)_x`-module injection

```text
B -> V_(1,x) tensor A
```

exists if and only if an injection

```text
K_q -> V_(q,x) tensor K_0                            (P23.11z3)
```

exists.  The same statements hold in `SU(2)_k` for `q<=k`, with tensor
products interpreted as fusion products and `S` interpreted as the
semisimple object

```text
direct-sum_(a+b=q-1) V_(a,x) tensor V_(b,y).
```

**Proof.**  The ordinary multiplicity-free decomposition

```text
S=direct-sum_(a+b=q-1) V_(a,x) tensor V_(b,y)
```

and Clebsch--Gordan give

```text
A=direct-sum_(s=0)^(q-1) V_(q-1-s,x) tensor K_s,

B=[direct-sum_(s=1)^q V_(q-s,x) tensor K_s]
  direct-sum
  [direct-sum_(s=0)^(q-2) V_(q-2-s,x) tensor K_s].
```

Fusion of the first line by `V_(1,x)` reproduces both displayed sums for
`B`.  The sole unmatched target is `V_(q,x) tensor K_0`, and the sole
unmatched source is `K_q`, proving `(P23.11z2)`.  Finite-dimensional
`SU(2)` modules are semisimple, so an injection exists exactly when every
irreducible target multiplicity dominates the corresponding source
multiplicity.  Cancelling the common character proves equivalence with
`(P23.11z3)`.

For `SU(2)_k`, every first-coordinate label in the displayed object `S` is
at most `q-1<=k-1`.  Therefore fusion by `V_1` has the same adjacent
branches, including the allowed top branch `V_k` when `q=k`; no omitted
summand crosses the affine wall.  The fusion category is semisimple, so the
same decomposition and multiplicity argument applies.  QED.

For the one-minus factor in `(P23.10)`, take

```text
K=product_i(V_(p_i,x) direct-sum V_(p_i,y)).
```

Thus all interior Clebsch--Gordan branches of the symmetric-power factor
cancel; the cross-support problem is exactly the boundary injection
`(P23.11z3)`.  The paired `H_1/H_2` terms are chain-level carriers for the
interior branches, while `K_q` is the support-marker boundary that remains
after their cancellation.  Proposition 23A8C describes only the optimistic
reduced carrier graph; `(P23.11z3)` and Lemma 23A6B show why its one-scalar
augmentation is insufficient for a full differential.

For a product of indexed plus factors, the boundary map has an exact
support-block description.  Put

```text
X_U=tensor_(i in U)V_(p_i,x),
M_s(U)=Hom_(SU(2)_y)(V_(s,y),tensor_(i in U)V_(p_i,y)).
```

The spaces `M_s(U)` carry no `SU(2)_x` action.

**Lemma 23A9A (boundary support blocks).**  For

```text
K=tensor_i(V_(p_i,x) direct-sum V_(p_i,y)),
```

one has

```text
K_s=direct-sum_(U subset [n]) M_s(U) tensor X_(U^c).  (P23.11z3a)
```

The block of a boundary map `(P23.11z3)` from source support `U` to
invariant target support `W` belongs exactly to

```text
Hom(M_q(U),M_0(W))
 tensor Hom_(SU(2)_x)(X_(U^c),V_q tensor X_(W^c)).    (P23.11z3b)
```

Thus a pure support transfer is the case `W=empty`: a path
`lambda:V_q -> X_U` and coevaluation give

```text
X_(U^c) -> V_q tensor X_U tensor X_(U^c).
```

A support-preserving channel swap is the case `W=U`; it requires both an
invariant path in `X_U` and a map
`X_(U^c) -> V_q tensor X_(U^c)`.

**Proof.**  Expanding the indexed direct-sum tensor product by the set of
factors assigned to the `y` coordinate gives `(P23.11z3a)`.  Taking
`SU(2)_x`-equivariant Hom between its `U` source summand and the `W` summand
of `V_q tensor K_0` gives `(P23.11z3b)`, because both multiplicity spaces
have trivial `SU(2)_x` action.  For `W=empty`, insert the coevaluation
`1 -> V_q tensor V_q`, apply `lambda` to the second copy, and tensor by the
identity of `X_(U^c)`.  The case `W=U` is the displayed specialization of
`(P23.11z3b)`.  QED.

The lemma characterizes all pure and support-preserving blocks, but
different source blocks may still have intersecting images.  Full
injectivity is the rank problem for their combined matrix, not ordinary
Hall on the list of nonzero Hom-spaces.

The smallest collision does admit a full boundary injection, but it uses
both a support-preserving channel swap and a pure support transfer.

**Proposition 23A9B (four-fundamental hybrid boundary injection).**  Let
`q=2` and

```text
K=(V_(1,x) direct-sum V_(1,y))^(tensor 4).
```

There is an explicit injection

```text
K_2 -> V_(2,x) tensor K_0.                            (P23.11z4)
```

It is valid in ordinary `SU(2)` and in every `SU(2)_k` with `k>=2`.

**Proof.**  Decompose by the indexed set `S` of factors assigned to the
`y` coordinate.  A `V_(2,y)` source occurs only for `|S|=2` or `|S|=4`.
For `|S|=2`, both the `V_2` path and the invariant path in
`V_1 tensor V_1` are unique.  The source over `S` is

```text
V_(1,x) tensor V_(1,x)=V_(0,x) direct-sum V_(2,x).
```

Use the `K_0` summand having the same `y`-support `S`.  Send its source
`V_0` branch to the unique `V_0` inside
`V_(2,x) tensor V_(2,x)`, and send its source `V_2` branch to
`V_(2,x) tensor V_(0,x)`.  These two maps are injective, and the six
indexed supports land in six different direct summands of `K_0`.

For `|S|=4`, the source multiplicity space is

```text
Hom(V_2,V_1^(tensor 4)).
```

Identify it, by exchanging the two copies of `SU(2)`, with the `V_2`
multiplicity space in the empty-`y`-support summand
`V_(1,x)^(tensor 4)` of `K_0`.  On every path copy use the unique invariant
map `V_2 tensor V_2 -> V_0`.  This image lies in the empty-support summand,
so it is disjoint from all six size-two images.  Their direct sum proves
`(P23.11z4)`.

The construction uses only the fusion rules

```text
V_1 star_k V_1=V_0 direct-sum V_2,
V_2 star_k V_2 contains V_0,
```

and exchange identifies the two fourfold path spaces at every `k>=2`.
At the truncation edge `k=2` one has

```text
V_1^(star_2 4)=2V_0 direct-sum 2V_2,
```

rather than the ordinary decomposition
`2V_0 direct-sum 3V_2 direct-sum V_4`; nevertheless the `V_2` source
path space and its exchanged empty-support target path space both have
dimension two.  Hence truncation changes their common multiplicity but
not the injection, and the same proof works in `SU(2)_k`.  QED.

In ordinary `SU(2)` the complete multiplicity audit is

```text
K_2=9V_0 direct-sum 6V_2,
K_0=10V_0 direct-sum 9V_2 direct-sum V_4,

V_2 tensor K_0
 =9V_0 direct-sum 20V_2 direct-sum 10V_4 direct-sum V_6. (P23.11z5)
```

Thus the `V_0` target capacity is exactly saturated.  Only three of its
nine copies come from the empty-support summand; the six nonempty
size-two supports are indispensable.  This is the representation-level
counterpart of the Schur-complement warning: a pure empty-support transfer
does not suffice, while the hybrid support-preserving swaps do.

There is a canonical-looking crystal realization of `(P23.11z3)`, but it
is not injective.  In the type-`A_1` highest-word model, fix an indexed
colour assignment and a highest path of the `y`-factors from zero to `q`.
Keep the factors through the last return of this path to zero in the
`y`-colour.  Reverse the terminal excursion, place it after an external
`B(q)`, and recolour that whole excursion `x`; it is then a weight-zero
component.  Finally use the unique two-factor `A_1` crystal commutors to
restore the original indexed factor order.  The retained `y` prefix is
invariant, so this rule does land in the crystal of `V_(q,x) tensor K_0`.

**Lemma 23A9C (last-return crystal-map obstruction).**  The preceding
last-return/commutor map is not injective, ordinarily or in `SU(2)_k` for
`k>=2`.  Its first collision has

```text
(p_1,p_2)=(1,1),                   q=1.              (P23.11z5a)
```

**Proof.**  Consider the two distinct indexed source highest words

```text
y_1=+, x_2=+,                 x_1=+, y_2=+.
```

Neither one has a nonempty invariant `y` prefix.  Reversing its singleton
terminal excursion and adjoining the external factor gives respectively

```text
+_q -_1 +_2,                 +_q -_2 +_1.
```

The first word is already in indexed order.  To order the second, commute
`-_2 tensor +_1`.  This word is the middle element of the `B(2)` component
of `B(1) tensor B(1)`, so the unique crystal commutor sends it to
`-_1 tensor +_2`.  Both indexed sources therefore have the same target

```text
+_q -_1 +_2.
```

The calculation uses only the components
`B(1) tensor B(1)=B(2) disjoint-union B(0)`, which are unchanged by level
`k>=2`.  QED.

This does not obstruct the boundary injection itself.  In this example
`V_1 tensor K_0` has more than enough copies of `V_1`; the failure is that
canonical switching forgets which equal indexed factor supplied the
terminal excursion.  A successful crystal map must retain that index by a
separate routing or make a noncanonical capacity allocation.

The minimal indexed repair works for `(P23.11z5a)`: keep the empty
`y`-support but use the terminal-excursion index as a tag, assigning the two
sources to the two weight-one components

```text
+_q+_1-_2,                    +_q-_1+_2
```

of `B(1) tensor B(1) tensor B(1)`.  Thus the boundary injection still
exists and the first collision is removed without changing the target
support.

That same-support Hall repair fails at the next arity.  For three
fundamental factors, `q=1`, retained `y`-support empty, and free
`x`-weight zero, there are four source components:

```text
three choices of one y-factor, with the other two x-factors invariant,
one all-y path + + - having no return to zero.         (P23.11z5b)
```

The corresponding target bin is the invariant part of four fundamental
`x`-factors, including the external one.  It has only the two ballot words

```text
+ + - -,                         + - + -.
```

Hence the tagged demand is four and the fixed-support capacity is two.
The other all-`y` path `+ - +` is not in this bin: its last zero retains
the first two indices.  Repairing `(P23.11z5b)` therefore requires spilling
capacity into nonempty invariant `y`-supports, rather than merely tagging
different `x` fusion components over the canonical last-return support.
Such a global spill does repair this example exactly.  There are five
weight-zero sources in total.  The target has the two empty-`y`-support
slots displayed above and one slot for each of the three indexed
two-factor invariant `y`-supports, also five in total.  The equality of
these global counts is an allocation, not a canonical crystal switching
rule; it is the first instance where the full indexed Hall pool is forced.

The strict C++ diagnostic
`character_ring_iter/analyze_su2_crystal_last_return.cpp` implements the
highest-path reversal and the unique two-factor commutor, and reproduces
`(P23.11z5a)`.  Its replay is
`certificates/su2_crystal_last_return.log`.  The displayed two-word
calculation is the obstruction; the program only checks the convention and
minimal search order.  The companion diagnostic
`character_ring_iter/analyze_su2_crystal_tagged_hall.cpp` groups source and
target highest paths by retained indexed support and endpoint weight; it
reproduces the exact fixed-support overload `4>2` and global repair
`5=5` in `(P23.11z5b)`.  Its replay is
`certificates/su2_crystal_tagged_hall.log`.

The global supplier has a precise support count.  For an indexed subset
`U`, write

```text
m_U(r)=dim Hom(V_r,X_U).
```

Fix an `x`-type `V_a`.  The source block with `y`-support `U` supplies

```text
d_U(a;q)=m_U(q)m_(U^c)(a)                            (P23.11z5c)
```

copies of `V_a`.  An invariant target support `W` supplies

```text
c_W(a;q)
 =m_W(0) sum_(t in a star_k q)m_(W^c)(t).             (P23.11z5d)
```

Indeed, the two factors in `(P23.11z5c)` select a `V_q` path in the
`y`-tensor product and a `V_a` path in the complementary `x` product.
For `(P23.11z5d)`, select an invariant `y` path and then a `V_a` path in
`V_q tensor X_(W^c)`.  Thus the boundary injection `(P23.11z3)` is, for
each `a`, exactly an indexed allocation of

```text
sum_U d_U(a;q)
```

source slots into the `sum_W c_W(a;q)` target slots.  Formula
`(P23.11z3b)` permits a scalar map between any selected source and target
copy of the same `V_a`; the hard assertion is that the global target count
always suffices.

A natural compression of this Hall pool is false.  Call an invariant
support `W` primitive if `W` is empty, or if `m_W(0)>0` but no proper
nonempty subset of `W` has an invariant.  One might hope to replace every
composite invariant target by its primitive invariant sub-supports and
prove

```text
sum_U d_U(a;q)
 <=sum_(W primitive) c_W(a;q).                        (P23.11z5e)
```

**Lemma 23A9D (primitive-supplier compression obstruction).**  Inequality
`(P23.11z5e)` fails ordinarily and in every `SU(2)_k`, `k>=2`.  Its first
ordinary obstruction is

```text
(p_1,p_2,p_3,p_4)=(1,1,1,2),       q=1,       a=0.
                                                               (P23.11z5f)
```

**Proof.**  The invariant supports are the empty set, the three indexed
pairs of label-one factors, and the three triples formed by such a pair
together with the label-two factor.  The first four are primitive.  Their
capacities are

```text
empty support:                         3,
three primitive label-one pairs:       3,
three composite (1,1,2) triples:       3.
```

The empty-support value follows from

```text
V_1^3 tensor V_2=3V_1 direct-sum 3V_3 direct-sum V_5.
```

Every other displayed support has invariant multiplicity one, and its
complement has one `V_1` path.  Complementing the seven invariant supports
also lists all source contributions in `(P23.11z5c)`.  Consequently

```text
sum_U d_U(0;1)=9,       primitive capacity=6,
full capacity=9.
```

At level two, `V_1^(star 3) star V_2=2V_1`; hence the corresponding three
numbers are `8,5,8`.  For every level at least three, the ordinary
`V_1` multiplicities used above are unchanged, so they remain `9,6,9`.
Thus the full `V_0` target is exactly saturated at every level, while
deleting the three composite suppliers creates a strict deficit.  QED.

Each composite triple in `(P23.11z5f)` contains a primitive invariant
label-one pair, but it is still an independent target summand: placing the
label-two index in the `y` support frees a different complementary
`x`-tensor product.  Hence no recursive compression to minimal invariant
sub-supports can prove `(P23.11z3)`.  A viable Hall argument must retain
composite invariant supports with their full indexed multiplicities.

The strict C++ diagnostic
`character_ring_iter/analyze_su2_boundary_primitive_suppliers.cpp`
constructs `(P23.11z5c)--(P23.11z5e)` from exact ordinary fusion paths and
finds `(P23.11z5f)` first in factor/label order.  Its replay is
`certificates/su2_boundary_primitive_suppliers.log`.  Minimality is
diagnostic; the displayed decomposition is the exact obstruction.

The three-fundamental overload `(P23.11z5b)` also rules out every
support-monotone switching, not only the canonical last-return rule.  Give
the source block over `U` access to every invariant target support

```text
W subset U.                                             (P23.11z5g)
```

This includes every rule which recolours old `y` factors to `x` and retains
an arbitrary invariant part of the old `y` support.

**Lemma 23A9E (support-monotone Hall obstruction).**  For three fundamental
factors, `q=1`, and `a=0`, the graph `(P23.11z5g)` has Hall deficit one
ordinarily and in every `SU(2)_k` with `k>=2`.  At level one its deficit is
two.

**Proof.**  Take the three singleton source supports.  Each has demand one:
its `y` factor is `V_1`, while its two `x` factors contain one invariant.
The only invariant subset of a singleton is the empty set.  Its scalar
target capacity is

```text
[V_0](V_1 tensor V_1^(tensor 3))=m_(1,1,1)(1)=2.
```

Thus demand three has neighborhood capacity two.  At level one the same
capacity is one.  QED.

The forced bidirectional spill can be realized without collisions in this
minimal case.

**Proposition 23A9F (three-fundamental bidirectional repair).**  The full
boundary injection for three fundamental factors and `q=1` exists
ordinarily and in every `SU(2)_k`.

**Proof.**  Index the factors cyclically by `0,1,2`.  Over a singleton
source `U={i}`, the source `x`-module is

```text
V_1 tensor V_1=V_0 direct-sum V_2,
```

with `V_2` omitted at level one.  Route it to
`W_i={i,i+1 mod 3}`.  The two `y` factors in `W_i` have their unique
invariant, while `V_1 tensor X_(W_i^c)=V_1 tensor V_1` has the same
displayed summands.  The three supports `W_i` are distinct.  Finally send
the all-`y` source path basis to the scalar copies in the empty-support
target.  Reciprocity identifies both multiplicities with
`m_(1,1,1)(1)`, and the empty support is disjoint from the preceding three
targets.  These are all source supports.  QED.

Here a singleton imports one old `x` index.  Even the enlargement which
allows arbitrary deletions and at most one such import,

```text
U -> W   if and only if   |W minus U|<=1,              (P23.11z5h)
```

does not close uniformly at the affine wall.

**Lemma 23A9G (one-import affine Hall obstruction).**  For every `k>=5`,
work in `SU(2)_k` and take

```text
(p_0,...,p_4)=(1,1,2,k-2,k),       q=a=k-1.           (P23.11z5i)
```

The one-import graph `(P23.11z5h)` has Hall deficit one, although the
unrestricted boundary coefficient is positive.

**Proof.**  Let `i` range over `0,1`.  The following source family has
total demand twelve:

```text
source support U             number of U     d_U(a;q) each
{i,3}                              2               1
{i,2,3}                            2               2
{i,4}                              2               2
{i,2,4}                            2               1.             (P23.11z5j)
```

The simple-current identity `V_k star_k V_r=V_(k-r)` and the ordinary
`A_1` fusion intervals give its complete positive neighborhood:

```text
target support W              empty   {0,1}   {0,1,2}   {2,3,4}
c_W(a;q)                         6        2         1          2.  (P23.11z5k)
```

Indeed,
`V_(k-1) star_k V_(k-1)=V_0 direct-sum V_2`, so here

```text
c_W(a;q)=m_W(0)(m_(W^c)(0)+m_(W^c)(2)).
```

This gives the four entries in `(P23.11z5k)` directly.  Checking the
invariant subsets which differ from a support in `(P23.11z5j)` by at most
one imported index shows that no other target has positive capacity.
Therefore the neighborhood capacity is `6+2+1+2=11<12`.

The unrestricted total source and target capacities are respectively
`12` and `14`; hence this coefficient of the boundary character is two.
Only the local support-routing rule fails.  QED.

The strict C++ diagnostic
`character_ring_iter/search_su2_boundary_support_monotone.cpp` constructs
the exact fusion multiplicities and capacitated flows.  It reproduces
Lemmas 23A9E and 23A9G and checks all support capacities in Proposition
23A9F.  Its replay is
`certificates/su2_boundary_support_monotone.log`.  The displayed fusion
calculations, rather than its bounded search modes, prove the statements.

There is also a genuinely unbounded outer chamber in which the complete
Hall allocation collapses to one support.  Assume all plus labels are
nontrivial, and put

```text
P=sum_i p_i,                         p_min=min_i p_i.
```

**Proposition 23A9H (single-source outer Hall chamber).**  If

```text
q>P-p_min,                                             (P23.11z5l)
```

then the boundary injection

```text
K_q -> V_(q,x) tensor K_0
```

exists ordinarily and in every `SU(2)_k` for which the displayed labels
exist.  In particular,

```text
q=P-2d,                         p_min>2d               (P23.11z5m)
```

allows arbitrary arity and arbitrary mixed labels.

**Proof.**  Every proper indexed support `U` has total label at most
`P-p_min<q`.  Its tensor product cannot contain `V_q`.  Finite fusion can
likewise not create such a label: every binary output satisfies
`c<=a+b`, so induction bounds every endpoint by the support's total label.
Thus the sole possibly nonzero source block in `(P23.11z3a)` is

```text
M_q([n]) tensor X_empty=M_q([n]) tensor V_0.
```

Use the empty-`y` target support.  By self-duality and rigidity, its `V_0`
multiplicity inside `V_q tensor X_[n]` is

```text
dim Hom(V_0,V_q tensor X_[n])
 =dim Hom(V_q,X_[n])
 =dim M_q([n]).
```

Choose dual path bases and map the source isomorphically onto this invariant
isotypic block.  Frobenius reciprocity is unchanged in the semisimple
fusion category `SU(2)_k`, so the finite statement requires no stability
or distance-from-wall condition.  Formula `(P23.11z5m)` is precisely
`(P23.11z5l)` with the fusion parity made explicit.  QED.

This chamber ends exactly when a proper support can carry `V_q`.
Coevaluation still embeds each such source block separately into the
empty-support target, but the images of different indexed blocks may
intersect.  Controlling those collisions is the unresolved unrestricted
Hall problem.

There is a general separation criterion for the pure transfers of
Lemma 23A9A.  For an object `Y`, let `Supp(Y)` be the set of simple labels
which occur in `Y`, and put

```text
A_q={U subset [n]:M_q(U)!=0}.                         (P23.11z5m0)
```

**Lemma 23A9H0 (isotypically separated pure transfers).**  If

```text
Supp(X_(U^c)) intersection Supp(X_(U'^c))=empty
for all distinct U,U' in A_q,                         (P23.11z5m0a)
```

then the boundary injection

```text
K_q -> V_(q,x) tensor K_0                             (P23.11z5m0b)
```

exists.  This holds both ordinarily and in every `SU(2)_k`.

**Proof.**  For each `U in A_q`, choose the `V_q`-isotypic inclusion

```text
M_q(U) tensor V_q -> X_U.
```

Coevaluation followed by this inclusion gives the pure-support
monomorphism

```text
M_q(U) tensor X_(U^c)
 -> M_q(U) tensor V_q tensor V_q tensor X_(U^c)
 -> V_q tensor X_[n].                                (P23.11z5m0c)
```

The last object is the empty-`y`-support summand of
`V_q tensor K_0`.  The image in `(P23.11z5m0c)` is isomorphic to its
source and hence has simple support `Supp(X_(U^c))`.  Condition
`(P23.11z5m0a)` places the images for distinct `U` in disjoint isotypic
parts of the target.  Their direct sum is therefore a monomorphism.  By
`(P23.11z3a)`, its source is all of `K_q`.  QED.

The first layer beyond the single-source chamber can also be separated
without choosing path bases.  For a plus list
`p_1,...,p_n`, `n>=2`, write

```text
P=sum_i p_i,                 p_(1)<=p_(2)<=...<=p_(n),
X_U=tensor_(i in U)V_(p_i),
I_q={i: Hom(V_q,X_([n] minus {i})) !=0}.              (P23.11z5m1)
```

All labels in the list are assumed nontrivial.

**Proposition 23A9H1 (complete co-singleton outer Hall chamber).**  Suppose

```text
q>P-p_(1)-p_(2)                                      (P23.11z5m2)
```

Then the boundary injection

```text
K_q -> V_(q,x) tensor K_0                            (P23.11z5m3)
```

exists ordinarily and in every `SU(2)_k` in which the displayed simples
exist, with no restriction on repetitions among the plus labels.

**Proof.**  If a support `U` omits at least two indices, then

```text
sum_(i in U)p_i<=P-p_(1)-p_(2)<q.
```

Every ordinary tensor constituent, and every finite-level fusion
constituent, has label at most the sum of its input labels.  Hence only the
full support and the co-singletons can contribute to `K_q`.  With
`M_q(U)=Hom(V_q,X_U)`, its complete source decomposition is therefore

```text
K_q=
 M_q([n]) tensor V_0
 direct-sum_(i in I_q)
 M_q([n] minus {i}) tensor V_(p_i).                   (P23.11z5m5)
```

The full-support source maps into the `V_0`-isotypic part of the
empty-`y` target `V_q tensor X_[n]`, exactly as in Proposition 23A9H.
Partition the active co-singletons by their omitted label.  For a fixed
label `p`, put

```text
I_q(p)={i in I_q:p_i=p},                 r_p=|I_q(p)|.
```

If two indexed factors have the same label, deleting either one gives
isomorphic tensor objects.  Hence either both co-singletons are active or
neither is, and `I_q(p)` contains every occurrence of `p` whenever it is
nonempty.

If `r_p=1`, send its source block to the `V_p`-isotypic part of the
empty-`y` target by the pure transfer `(P23.11z5m0c)`.  If `r_p=2`, do
this for one of the two source blocks.  For the other, use the target
support `W` consisting of the two indexed copies of `p`.  It is an
invariant target support because

```text
dim Hom(V_0,V_p tensor V_p)=1.                        (P23.11z5m6)
```

More generally, if `r_p>=3`, cyclically order
`I_q(p)={i_1,...,i_(r_p)}` and assign the source indexed by `i_j` to

```text
W_j={i_j,i_(j+1)},                  i_(r_p+1)=i_1.    (P23.11z5m7)
```

These are `r_p` distinct indexed equal-pair target supports, and every
assignment is incident to its source index.  For a source indexed by `i`
and its assigned pair `W`, rigidity and commutativity give

```text
dim Hom(V_p,V_q tensor X_(W^c))
 =dim Hom(V_q,V_p tensor X_(W^c))
 =dim Hom(V_q,X_([n] minus {i})).                     (P23.11z5m8)
```

The last equality holds because `W` removes two copies of `V_p`, while
the tensor factor displayed on the middle line restores one; the result is
isomorphic to the tensor product with one indexed copy of `V_p` removed.
Thus the `V_p`-isotypic part of the target summand indexed by `W` receives
the whole source block assigned to it.

Distinct `W` are distinct direct summands of `K_0`.  The only
co-singleton blocks still sent to the empty-`y` target use at most one
copy of each simple type `V_p`; different label classes use disjoint
isotypic parts, and all are disjoint from the `V_0` part used by the full
support.  The displayed maps therefore assemble to `(P23.11z5m3)`.
Self-duality, rigidity, and `(P23.11z5m6)` hold unchanged in
`SU(2)_k`, so the construction is uniform in the level.  QED.

This chamber strictly extends Proposition 23A9H.  For example, in ordinary
`SU(2)` take plus labels `[1,3,5]` and `q=6`.  Both co-singletons omitting
labels one and three carry `V_6`, so the old single-source condition fails.
Proposition 23A9H1 separates their `V_1` and `V_3` source types.  It also
covers repeated active types: equal-pair invariant target supports pay all
collisions beyond the one copy retained in the empty-`y` target.  For
example, `[1,1,1,4]` with `q=6` has three active co-singletons of type
`V_1`; the three indexed pairs among the label-one factors receive them
separately.  This example remains valid already in `SU(2)_6`.

For larger complementary supports, coevaluation must be resolved by simple
channels.  The unevaluated map
`Hom(V_q,Z tensor A) tensor A -> V_q tensor Z` need not be injective when
`A` is reducible.  For example, ordinarily take

```text
A=V_1 tensor V_1=V_0 direct-sum V_2,       Z=V_0,       q=2.
```

The Hom-space has dimension one, so the proposed source is
`V_0 direct-sum V_2`, while the target is only `V_2`.

**Lemma 23A9H2 (channelwise collision-pairing criterion).**  Let `C,D` be
disjoint indexed supports and suppose

```text
X_C isomorphic to X_D isomorphic to A,
A=direct-sum_s a_s V_s,                    Z=X_((C union D)^c).  (P23.11z5m9)
```

Put

```text
h_s=dim Hom(V_q,Z tensor V_s),
M=sum_s a_s h_s,
Delta_A=dim Hom(V_0,A tensor A)=sum_s a_s^2.          (P23.11z5m10)
```

If

```text
M a_s<=Delta_A h_s                 for every s,       (P23.11z5m11)
```

then the source block with complement `C` embeds into the target block
whose invariant support is `W=C union D`.  Together with the pure transfer
of the source block with complement `D` into the empty-support target, this
gives

```text
[M_q(C^c) tensor X_C] direct-sum
[M_q(D^c) tensor X_D]
 -> V_q tensor [X_[n] direct-sum
       M_0(C union D) tensor X_((C union D)^c)].       (P23.11z5m12)
```

The criterion is automatic if `A` is simple.  It also holds with equality
if `A` is multiplicity-free and the values `h_s` are constant on
`Supp(A)`.

**Proof.**  Since `D^c=Z union C` and `C^c=Z union D`, both source
multiplicity spaces have dimension

```text
dim Hom(V_q,Z tensor A)=sum_s a_s h_s=M.              (P23.11z5m13)
```

Thus either source object has `V_s` multiplicity `M a_s`.  The invariant
support `W=C union D` has

```text
dim M_0(W)=dim Hom(V_0,A tensor A)=Delta_A.
```

Its corresponding summand in `V_q tensor K_0` is
`Delta_A` copies of `V_q tensor Z`.  By rigidity, the multiplicity of
`V_s` in that target is `Delta_A h_s`.  Inequality
`(P23.11z5m11)` therefore gives an injection of the first source block into
the `W` target, simple type by simple type.  Lemma 23A9A gives the pure
injection of the second source block into the empty-support target.  Those
are distinct indexed summands of `K_0`, proving `(P23.11z5m12)`.

If `A=V_a` is simple, then `M=h_a`, `Delta_A=1`, and
`(P23.11z5m11)` is equality.  If `A` is multiplicity-free with `d` simple
constituents and every supported `h_s` equals `h`, then
`M=dh` and `Delta_A=d`, again giving equality.  QED.

**Corollary 23A9H2A (flat-profile repeated co-doubletons).**  Let `C,D`
be disjoint two-index complements carrying the same unordered label pair
`{a,b}`.  Put

```text
Z=X_((C union D)^c),             I=a star_k b.
```

If

```text
dim Hom(V_q,Z tensor V_s)=h              for every s in I,       (P23.11z5m13a)
```

then the two corresponding co-doubleton source blocks admit the injection
`(P23.11z5m12)`.  Here `star_k` is replaced by the ordinary
Clebsch--Gordan interval in stable `SU(2)`.

**Proof.**  The product `A=V_a tensor V_b` is the multiplicity-free direct
sum of the simples indexed by `I`, both ordinarily and at finite level.
The constant-profile case of Lemma 23A9H2 applies.  QED.

The simple case can close an arbitrarily large repeated collision family
without reusing the empty-support target.

**Corollary 23A9H3 (repeated simple-complement cycle).**  Let
`C_1,...,C_r` be pairwise disjoint nonempty indexed supports such that

```text
X_(C_j) isomorphic to V_a                 (1<=j<=r).   (P23.11z5m14)
```

The direct sum of their boundary source blocks admits an injection into the
empty-support target and the invariant target supports
`C_i union C_j`.  More precisely:

1. if `r=1`, use the pure transfer to the empty-support target;
2. if `r=2`, use `(P23.11z5m12)`;
3. if `r>=3`, cyclically index the supports and send the source with
   complement `C_j` to the target support

```text
W_j=C_j union C_(j+1),                  C_(r+1)=C_1.  (P23.11z5m15)
```

In the last case the `r` target supports are distinct, so no
empty-support capacity is used.  The statement holds ordinarily and in
every finite fusion category `SU(2)_k`.

**Proof.**  The first two assertions are the pure transfer and the simple
case of Lemma 23A9H2.  For `r>=3`, the supports in each consecutive pair
are disjoint and both carry the same self-dual simple `V_a`.  Hence
`M_0(W_j)` contains the scalar channel in `V_a tensor V_a`, and the simple
case of Lemma 23A9H2 sends the complete `C_j` source block to the `W_j`
target.  Pairwise disjointness and `r>=3` make the indexed unions `W_j`
distinct.  The direct sum of these maps is the required injection.  QED.

**Corollary 23A9H4 (simple-current co-doubleton family).**  In `SU(2)_k`,

```text
V_k tensor V_b=V_(k-b).                               (P23.11z5m16)
```

Hence any pairwise disjoint family of active two-index complements, each
consisting of one label `k` and one fixed label `b`, satisfies Corollary
23A9H3.  For three or more such complements, its complete source family is
paid by distinct four-index invariant supports, without using an empty or
two-index target support.

If `n>=3` and

```text
q>P-p_(1)-p_(2)-p_(3),                               (P23.11z5m17)
```

then these families belong to the first unresolved complement layer after
the full and co-singleton supports: every active source omits at most two
factors.

**Proof.**  The simple-current identity `(P23.11z5m16)` makes the object
on each displayed two-index complement the same self-dual simple
`V_(k-b)`.  Corollary 23A9H3 applies.  When the family has at least three
members, its cyclic targets are unions of two disjoint two-index
complements and therefore have four indices; this proves the target-support
claim.  Finally, a support omitting at least three indices has total label
at most `P-p_(1)-p_(2)-p_(3)<q`, so it cannot carry `V_q`, ordinarily or
after affine truncation.  QED.

Corollary 23A9H4 is a source-family closure, not a closure of every
co-doubleton type under `(P23.11z5m17)`.  Reducible complementary objects
must still satisfy the channel capacities `(P23.11z5m11)` or use additional
target supports.

The Hall cuts for a bounded-import graph admit an exact compression.  For
a set family `D subset 2^[n]`, define its `r`th upper shadow by

```text
D^(+r)={W: there is S in D with S subset W and |W minus S|<=r}.   (P23.11z5n)
```

**Lemma 23A9I (down-set Hall reduction).**  Fix `q,a` and give the support
graph the edge rule

```text
U -> W   if and only if   |W minus U|<=r.              (P23.11z5o)
```

With the exact indexed capacities `(P23.11z5c)--(P23.11z5d)`, this graph
has a full capacitated matching if and only if, for every down-set
`D subset 2^[n]`,

```text
sum_(U in D)d_U(a;q)
 <=sum_(W in D^(+r))c_W(a;q).                         (P23.11z5p)
```

Moreover `D^(+r)` is itself a down-set.  In particular, radius two is
equivalent to the weighted second-shadow inequalities `(P23.11z5p)`;
this is a reduction, not a proof of their positivity.

**Proof.**  Let `X` be an arbitrary family of source supports and put
`D=downarrow X`.  Its neighborhood is exactly `D^(+r)`.  Indeed, if
`U in X` reaches `W`, then `S=U intersection W` belongs to `D` and

```text
|W minus S|=|W minus U|<=r.
```

Conversely, if `S in D`, `S subset W`, and `|W minus S|<=r`, choose
`U in X` with `S subset U`.  Then
`W minus U subset W minus S`, so `U` reaches `W`.

Adding every source support in `D` to `X` leaves this neighborhood
unchanged and can only increase its nonnegative demand.  Hence the
strongest Hall inequality having down-closure `D` is precisely
`(P23.11z5p)`.  Every source family has such a down-closure, while taking
`X=D` proves the converse.  The capacitated Hall theorem now gives the
equivalence.

Finally, if `W in D^(+r)` and `T subset W`, choose a witnessing `S in D`
and put `S'=S intersection T`.  Downward closure gives `S' in D`, while

```text
|T minus S'|<=|W minus S|<=r.
```

Thus `T in D^(+r)`.  QED.

Taking `D=2^[n]` in `(P23.11z5p)` recovers the unrestricted boundary
multiplicity inequality itself.  Therefore the second-shadow formulation
does not evade the one-minus positivity problem; it isolates the additional
local-routing cuts which would have to be controlled after total
positivity.

Rigidity gives the useful invariant-network form

```text
d_U(a;q)=m_(U union {q})(0)m_(U^c union {a})(0),
c_W(a;q)=m_W(0)m_(W^c union {q,a})(0).                (P23.11z5q)
```

Here the braces denote new external indexed leaves, even when their labels
coincide with old ones.  Thus the radius-two inequality asks for a weighted
switching between two invariant spin networks: move the distinguished
`q` leaf from the first component to the component containing `a`, while
importing at most two old leaves into the first component.  Ordinary
polygon feasibility of such a switch would not prove `(P23.11z5p)`;
the required assertion preserves the full products of indexed path
multiplicities in `(P23.11z5q)`.

Literal standard-monomial switching does not preserve the fused blocks in
this formulation.  The obstruction already occurs on one four-vertex
Plucker face.  For ordered vertices `p<u<v<r`, put

```text
C=[uv]_[P][pr]_[N],       A=[pv]_[P][ur]_[N],
B=[pu]_[P][vr]_[N].                                  (P23.11z5r)
```

After forgetting the two colours, the Plucker relation is `C=A-B`.

**Lemma 23A9J (whole-block Plucker-switching obstruction).**  Let the four
vertices in `(P23.11z5r)` be fused blocks `V_delta`, with `delta>=2`.
Expanding them into `delta` fundamental occurrences and applying the
ordinary Plucker/jeu-de-taquin switch cannot be projected to a
multiplication-compatible triangular switch supported only on
whole-block cut tableaux, even when both active old blocks may change
colour.

More precisely, the two switched whole-block cuts on this face are
`A^delta` and `B^delta`, whereas

```text
C^delta
 =sum_(j=0)^delta (-1)^j binom(delta,j)
    A^(delta-j)B^j.                                  (P23.11z5s)
```

Every intermediate term `0<j<delta` splits both blocks `u` and `v`
between the two colours, and none of these terms can be eliminated by
further Plucker straightening inside the span of the two whole-block
cuts.

**Proof.**  In `A`, the block `v` belongs to colour `P` and `u` to colour
`N`; in `B` these assignments are reversed.  Hence
`A^(delta-j)B^j` gives positive degree in both colours at both `u` and
`v` when `0<j<delta`, so it is not a cut of the indexed `V_delta`
blocks.

The intermediate monomials in `(P23.11z5s)` are linearly independent.
Indeed, evaluate the four bracket vectors at

```text
v_p=(1,0),       v_r=(0,s),       v_u=(1,1),
v_v=(1,t).
```

Then

```text
A=st,                 B=s,                 C=s(t-1).
```

Thus a relation
`sum_j gamma_j A^(delta-j)B^j=0` would give
`s^delta sum_j gamma_j t^(delta-j)=0` for all `s,t`, forcing every
`gamma_j` to vanish.  Consequently `(P23.11z5s)` cannot be replaced by a
linear combination of only `A^delta` and `B^delta`.

At `delta=2` the obstruction is the explicit identity

```text
C^2=A^2-2AB+B^2.                                    (P23.11z5t)
```

Switching the first occurrence by `A` and the second by `B`, or vice
versa, produces the same symmetrized term `AB`.  This is the symbolic
collision: the occurrence tag is lost on projection to `V_2`, while the
nonzero middle term is indispensable by the preceding independence.
QED.

Lemma 23A9J rules out only multiplication-compatible whole-block
Plucker/JDT switching.  It does not obstruct the boundary injection
`(P23.11z3)`, nor a switch in a fused Temperley--Lieb/recoupling basis:
such a basis retains the intermediate channels which the cut projection
in `(P23.11z5s)` discards.  In particular, the radius-two Hall evidence
below cannot be promoted to a proof by applying ordinary jeu de taquin to
the fundamental occurrences and forgetting their occurrence tags.

The compressed OpenMP diagnostic
`character_ring_iter/search_su2_boundary_radius2_threaded.cpp` constructs
the exact source and target capacities, represents deletion followed by at
most two imports as a layered subset-lattice flow network, and computes its
full capacitated rank.  Its replay is
`certificates/su2_boundary_radius2_threaded.log`.  The recorded runs use 48
threads selected from the available CPU, RAM, and machine-load budget.
They found no radius-two Hall deficit through the declared randomized
finite-wall ranges, including words of length fifteen at level thirty.
This is diagnostic evidence only; it proves neither `(P23.11z5p)` nor the
boundary injection.

There is an exact fused-leaf recurrence which isolates what a
Temperley--Lieb recoupling induction would still have to control.  Let
`N_p` denote the ordinary fusion matrix, or the fusion matrix of
`SU(2)_k`, and let

```text
F(r,s)=[V_r box-times V_s]
       product_i((V_(p_i) box-times 1) direct-sum
                 (1 box-times V_(p_i))).
```

The table `F` is symmetric.  Define its boundary defect and its oriented
three-terminal transfer defect by

```text
B_F(q,a)=sum_(t in q star_k a)F(0,t)-F(q,a),

C_F(p,q;a)
 =sum_(t in q star_k a)F(p,t)
  -sum_(u in p star_k q)F(u,a).                       (P23.11z5y)
```

Adjoining one indexed plus leaf `p` replaces `F` by

```text
F^[p]=N_p F+F N_p.                                   (P23.11z5z)
```

**Lemma 23A9L (fused-leaf quadrilateral recurrence).**  Uniformly in the
ordinary and finite fusion rings,

```text
B_(F^[p])(q,a)
 =sum_(v in p star_k a)B_F(q,v)+C_F(p,q;a),           (P23.11z5za)

C_F(a,q;p)=-C_F(p,q;a).                              (P23.11z5zb)
```

Consequently the two opposite fused-leaf updates obey the paired identity

```text
B_(F^[p])(q,a)+B_(F^[a])(q,p)
 =2 sum_(v in p star_k a)B_F(q,v).                    (P23.11z5zc)
```

**Proof.**  Formula `(P23.11z5z)` gives

```text
F^[p](0,t)=F(p,t)+sum_v N_(p,t)^v F(0,v),
F^[p](q,a)=sum_u N_(p,q)^u F(u,a)
            +sum_v N_(p,a)^v F(q,v).
```

After summing the first line over `t in q star_k a`, fusion associativity
identifies

```text
sum_(t,v)N_(q,a)^t N_(p,t)^v F(0,v)
 =sum_v N_(p,a)^v sum_w N_(q,v)^w F(0,w).
```

Subtracting the second line gives `(P23.11z5za)`.  If

```text
T_F(p,q,a)=sum_t N_(q,a)^t F(p,t),
```

then `T_F` is symmetric in its last two arguments and

```text
C_F(p,q;a)=T_F(p,q,a)-T_F(a,p,q).
```

This proves `(P23.11z5zb)`; adding the two instances of
`(P23.11z5za)` proves `(P23.11z5zc)`.  QED.

This is a genuinely fused-channel statement: all intermediate labels of
the new `V_p` leaf have already been summed with their exact fusion
multiplicities, including affine-wall truncation.  It does not project a
divided-power Plucker switch back to whole-block cuts.  It also gives a
sharp no-go for a leafwise positivity induction.  The remainder
`C_F(p,q;a)` has no fixed sign.  Already for the one-leaf background

```text
F=(V_(1,x) direct-sum V_(1,y)),       (p,q,a)=(2,2,1)
```

in the ordinary ring, one has

```text
C_F(2,2;1)=-1.                                       (P23.11z5zd)
```

Indeed the first sum in `(P23.11z5y)` is zero, whereas the second contains
the unique channel `0 in 2 star 2` and `F(0,1)=1`.  The same calculation
works in `SU(2)_k` for every `k>=2`.  Thus a proof cannot make every local
fused remainder positive by a choice of `F`-move gauges or triangular
ordering: the opposite orientation has the negated remainder by
`(P23.11z5zb)`.  What recoupling does provide is the exact cancellation
`(P23.11z5zc)` across opposite updates.

**Corollary 23A9M (exact oscillation criterion and
minimal-counterexample restriction).**  Put

```text
R_F(p,q,a)=sum_(v in p star_k a)B_F(q,v).
```

If `R_F(p,q,a)>=0`, the two opposite updated boundary defects in
`(P23.11z5zc)` are both nonnegative if and only if

```text
|C_F(p,q;a)|
 <=R_F(p,q,a).                                        (P23.11z5ze)
```

Equivalently, put

```text
Theta_B(p,q,a)=sum_(u in p star_k q)B_F(u,a).
```

Then `(P23.11z5ze)` is exactly the fusion-triangle inequality

```text
|Theta_B(p,q,a)-Theta_B(q,a,p)|
 <=Theta_B(p,a,q).                                    (P23.11z5zf)
```

In particular, in a counterexample having the fewest indexed plus leaves,
neither external label `q` nor external label `a` can occur among the plus
labels.

**Proof.**  Equations `(P23.11z5za)--(P23.11z5zb)` identify the two
updated defects with `R_F+C_F` and `R_F-C_F`; for nonnegative `R_F`, both
are nonnegative exactly when `(P23.11z5ze)` holds.

For the triangle form, write `G(t)=F(0,t)`.  The boundary definition gives

```text
F(r,s)=sum_t N_(r,s)^t G(t)-B_F(r,s).
```

Substitution in `(P23.11z5y)` cancels the two `G` terms by fusion
associativity and leaves

```text
C_F(p,q;a)=Theta_B(p,q,a)-Theta_B(q,a,p),
R_F(p,q,a)=Theta_B(p,a,q).
```

This proves the equivalence with `(P23.11z5zf)`.

Now suppose a shortest counterexample contains a plus leaf `p=a`.  Its
shorter background has all boundary defects nonnegative, while
`C_F(a,q;a)=0` by `(P23.11z5zb)`.  Equation `(P23.11z5za)` makes the
claimed counterexample nonnegative, a contradiction.  Since
`F(q,a)=F(a,q)` and `q star_k a=a star_k q`, the definition
`(P23.11z5y)` gives `B_F(q,a)=B_F(a,q)`.  The same argument after
exchanging the two external labels excludes a plus leaf `p=q`.  QED.

The oriented remainder also has an exact determinant interpretation in the
fused `USp(4)` basis.  In the ordinary ring put

```text
P(x,y)=product_i[chi_(p_i)(x)+chi_(p_i)(y)],
dnu_1^norm=(1/2)(x-y)^2 dmu(x)dmu(y),

E_(p,a)(x,y)
 =[chi_p(x)chi_a(y)-chi_a(x)chi_p(y)]/(x-y).
```

**Lemma 23A9N (symplectic determinant factorization and sign no-go).**
For all `p,q,a`,

```text
C_F(p,q;a)
 =-integral D_q E_(p,a) P dnu_1^norm,                 (P23.11z5zg)
```

and

```text
E_(p,a)=
  Psi_(p-1,a),              p>a;
  0,                         p=a;
 -Psi_(a-1,p),              p<a.                     (P23.11z5zh)
```

Nevertheless the proposed orientation rule

```text
C_F(p,q;a)(p-a)<=0                                  (P23.11z5zi)
```

is false.  Its one-leaf counterexample is

```text
P=S_2,                  (p,q,a)=(2,1,1),             (P23.11z5zj)
```

for which `C_F(2,1;1)=1`.  The same fusion calculation is valid in every
`SU(2)_k`, `k>=2`.  Together with `(P23.11z5zd)`, where `p>a` but
`C_F=-1`, this shows that neither orientation can depend only on the sign
of `p-a`.

**Proof.**  Character orthogonality writes the two terms of
`(P23.11z5y)` as

```text
sum_(t in q star a)F(p,t)
 =integral chi_p(x)chi_q(y)chi_a(y)P(x,y)dmu(x)dmu(y),

sum_(u in p star q)F(u,a)
 =integral chi_p(x)chi_q(x)chi_a(y)P(x,y)dmu(x)dmu(y).
```

Since `P` is symmetric in `x,y`, antisymmetrizing the remaining factor
gives

```text
C_F(p,q;a)
 =-(1/2)integral [chi_q(x)-chi_q(y)]
   [chi_p(x)chi_a(y)-chi_a(x)chi_p(y)]P dmu(x)dmu(y).
```

Using

```text
D_q=[chi_q(x)-chi_q(y)]/(x-y)
```

proves `(P23.11z5zg)`.  Formula `(P23.11z5zh)` is the `USp(4)` Weyl
determinant formula for `Psi_(r,s)`, with the two rows exchanged when
`p<a`.

For `(P23.11z5zj)`, the only nonzero entries of the one-leaf table are

```text
F(2,0)=F(0,2)=1.
```

The first sum in `C_F(2,1;1)` runs over `1 star_k 1` and contains the
channel zero, so it equals one.  The second runs over `2 star_k 1` and
equals zero.  Thus `C_F=1` although `p-a=1`.  Equivalently,

```text
D_1=Psi_(0,0),        E_(2,1)=Psi_(1,1),
S_2=Psi_(2,0)-Psi_(1,1)+Psi_(0,0),
```

so `(P23.11z5zg)` gives `C_F=-(-1)=1` directly by `USp(4)`
orthogonality.  QED.

**Lemma 23A9Q (disjoint affine no-margin family).**  In `SU(2)_3`, let
`F_n` be the coefficient table of the plus background `S_2^n`.  For every
`n>=0`,

```text
R_(F_n)(2,1,1)-|C_(F_n)(2,1;1)|=1.                  (P23.11z5zj1)
```

For `n>=1` the denominator `R_(F_n)(2,1,1)` tends to infinity.  Hence

```text
|C_(F_n)(2,1;1)|/R_(F_n)(2,1,1) -> 1                (P23.11z5zj2)
```

strictly from below.  This family obeys the minimal-counterexample
restriction in Corollary 23A9M: the updated plus word contains only label
two, whereas `q=a=1`.  Thus no proof of `(P23.11z5ze)` can use a uniform
positive additive gap larger than the integral unit, or a uniform relative
gap away from one.

**Proof.**  Only the even labels `0,2` occur in `F_n`.  Write its even block
as

```text
F_n|_(0,2) = [[x_n,y_n],[y_n,z_n]].
```

On this block,

```text
N_2=[[0,1],[1,1]],
```

and the plus update `F_(n+1)=N_2F_n+F_nN_2` gives

```text
x_(n+1)=2y_n,
y_(n+1)=x_n+y_n+z_n,
z_(n+1)=2y_n+2z_n.                                  (P23.11z5zj3)
```

Since `(x_0,y_0,z_0)=(1,0,0)`, subtraction in `(P23.11z5zj3)` shows

```text
x_n+y_n-z_n=1                                       (P23.11z5zj4)
```

for every `n`.  At `(p,q,a)=(2,1,1)`, the `SU(2)_3` fusion rules give

```text
C_(F_n)(2,1;1)=y_n+z_n,
R_(F_n)(2,1,1)
 =B_(F_n)(1,1)+B_(F_n)(1,3)=x_n+2y_n.
```

Their difference is `(P23.11z5zj4)`.  Finally all three sequences are
nonnegative, and `(P23.11z5zj3)` gives
`y_(n+2)>=2y_n` once `y_1=1`; hence `R_(F_n)>=2y_n` is unbounded.  This
proves `(P23.11z5zj1)--(P23.11z5zj2)`.  QED.

The exact C++ diagnostic
`character_ring_iter/search_su2_boundary_oscillation.cpp` constructs every
actual plus-word table with arbitrary-precision integer arithmetic and
tests `(P23.11z5ze)`, the individual background defects, the disjoint
restriction, and the rejected sign rule `(P23.11z5zi)`.  Its finite replay
covered the complete rectangle `1<=k<=16`, background length `0<=n<=9`,
every multiset word with labels in `1,...,k`, every `1<=p,q<=k`, and every
`0<=a<=k`: `5,311,734` backgrounds and `18,132,703,200` exact triples.
No oscillation deficit or negative background defect occurred.  The
ordinary replay through label ten and length nine covered `101,615,800`
exact triples and likewise found no oscillation deficit.  The replay and
the disjoint extremal data are recorded in
`certificates/su2_boundary_oscillation_summary.log`.  These bounded searches
are diagnostic only; Lemma 23A9Q, not the search, proves the unbounded
no-margin statement.

The finite sine transform gives a sharp obstruction to inheriting the
boundary inequality term by term from a cyclic or dihedral character
inequality.  Put `N=k+2`,

```text
theta_j=(j+1)pi/N,                     0<=j<=k,
mu_j=(2/N)sin(theta_j)^2,
c_r(theta)=2cos(r theta).
```

Thus `mu` is the `SU(2)_k` spectral measure at `V_0`; it is the Weyl
`h^2` transform with `h(theta)=sin(theta)`.  Pointwise,

```text
c_1=chi_1,                    c_3=chi_3-chi_1.
```

**Lemma 23A9P (two-minus Weyl-transform no-go).**  For every `k>=3` and
every `1<=r<=k-2`, the uniform cyclic average on the `2N`th roots
satisfies

```text
E_cyc[(c_r(x)-c_r(y))(c_(r+2)(x)-c_(r+2)(y))]=0,     (P23.11z5zk)
```

whereas its Weyl `h^2` transform satisfies

```text
E_(mu tensor mu)[
 (c_r(x)-c_r(y))(c_(r+2)(x)-c_(r+2)(y))]=-2.         (P23.11z5zl)
```

Hence the Weyl/Doob transform does not preserve even the specific
two-minus cyclic GKS inequality.  In the smallest case, regrouping the
cyclic constituents into the complete `SU(2)` character restores
nonnegativity:

```text
chi_3=c_3+c_1,

E_(mu tensor mu)[
 (chi_1(x)-chi_1(y))(chi_3(x)-chi_3(y))]
 =-2+2=0.                                            (P23.11z5zm)
```

**Proof.**  On the cyclic group of order `2N>=10`, the bounds
`1<=r<=N-4` imply that `c_r,c_(r+2)` have zero means and are distinct
noninverse real characters.  They are therefore orthogonal.  The
elementary covariance identity

```text
E[(f(x)-f(y))(g(x)-g(y))]
 =2{E[fg]-E[f]E[g]}
```

therefore proves `(P23.11z5zk)`.

For the sine measure use, for every `r>=1`,

```text
c_r=chi_r-chi_(r-2),                 chi_s=0 for s<0.
```

Character orthogonality in `SU(2)_k` gives

```text
E_mu[c_r c_(r+2)]
 =tau_k[(chi_r-chi_(r-2))(chi_(r+2)-chi_r)]=-1.
```

Moreover `E_mu[c_r]=0` except at `r=2`, where it is `-1`, while
`E_mu[c_(r+2)]=0` throughout the stated range.  Hence

```text
Cov_mu(c_r,c_(r+2))=-1,
```

and the covariance identity proves `(P23.11z5zl)`.  For the regrouping
example `r=1`, the displayed identities specialize to

```text
E_mu[c_1]=E_mu[c_3]=0,
E_mu[c_1c_3]=tau_k[chi_1(chi_3-chi_1)]=-1.
```

Finally `E_mu[c_1^2]=1`, so the added diagonal term in
`chi_3=c_3+c_1` contributes two and proves `(P23.11z5zm)`.  QED.

The fusion-triangle criterion itself closes uniformly for the first three
updated plus leaves.  Write

```text
I(b_1,...,b_m)=dim Hom(1,V_(b_1) star_k ... star_k V_(b_m)),
```

with the ordinary tensor product in the stable ring.  All labels below are
nontrivial.

**Proposition 23A9R (two-leaf fusion-triangle base theorem).**  Let `F`
be the plus table of a background containing at most two indexed leaves,
adjoin a further leaf `p`, and assume that the two external labels satisfy

```text
q,a notin {all background labels,p}.                 (P23.11z5zn)
```

Then the fusion-triangle inequality `(P23.11z5zf)` holds, ordinarily and
in every `SU(2)_k` containing the displayed labels.  More precisely:

1.  For the empty background,

```text
C_F(p,q;a)=0,          R_F(p,q,a)=N_(p,q)^a.          (P23.11z5zo)
```

2.  For the one-leaf background `[r]`,

```text
C_F(p,q;a)=indicator_(q=a)indicator_(p=r),
R_F(p,q,a)=I(p,q,a,r).                               (P23.11z5zp)
```

3.  For the two-leaf background `[r,s]`,

```text
C_F(p,q;a)
 =indicator_(q=a)N_(r,s)^p
  +indicator_(p=s)N_(q,a)^r
  +indicator_(p=r)N_(q,a)^s,

R_F(p,q,a)
 =I(p,q,a,r,s)+indicator_(r=s)N_(p,a)^q.             (P23.11z5zq)
```

Thus equality in `(P23.11z5ze)` occurs exactly when the two quantities on
the corresponding right sides of `(P23.11z5zo)--(P23.11z5zq)` are equal.
In particular, in the one-leaf case it is equivalent to

```text
I(p,q,a,r)=indicator_(q=a)indicator_(p=r).            (P23.11z5zr)
```

If the indicator vanishes, this says exactly that the fusion intervals
`p star_k q` and `a star_k r` are disjoint.  If the indicator is one,
ordinary equality never occurs, while finite-level equality occurs exactly
when `p=k` or `q=k`.

**Proof.**  With no background leaves,

```text
B_F(u,v)=indicator_(u=v>0).
```

Therefore all three cyclic `Theta_B` values equal the symmetric fusion
coefficient `N_(p,q)^a`, proving `(P23.11z5zo)`.

For one background leaf, direct expansion of its two colour assignments
gives, for `v>0`,

```text
B_F(u,v)=N_(u,v)^r-indicator_(u=0)indicator_(v=r).
```

Consequently

```text
Theta_B(x,y,z)
 =I(x,y,z,r)-indicator_(x=y)indicator_(z=r).          (P23.11z5zs)
```

Apply `(P23.11z5zs)` to the three cyclic orders and use
`(P23.11z5zn)`.  This gives `(P23.11z5zp)`.  If its indicator is one,
pair the two copies of `q=a` and the two copies of `p=r` to obtain an
invariant path, so `I(p,q,a,r)>=1`.  Hence `C_F<=R_F`, and the equality
statement is immediate.  Since fusion is multiplicity-free,

```text
I(p,q,a,r)=|(p star_k q) intersection (a star_k r)|.
```

When `q=a,p=r`, this cardinality is

```text
min(p,q,k-p,k-q)+1
```

at finite level and `min(p,q)+1` ordinarily.  The labels are nontrivial,
which proves the refined equality assertions.

For two background leaves, their four indexed colour assignments give,
for `z>0`,

```text
B_F(u,z)
 =I(u,z,r,s)+indicator_(u=z)indicator_(r=s)
  -indicator_(u=0)N_(r,s)^z
  -indicator_(u=r)indicator_(z=s)
  -indicator_(u=s)indicator_(z=r).
```

Summing over `u in x star_k y` yields

```text
Theta_B(x,y,z)
 =I(x,y,z,r,s)+indicator_(r=s)N_(x,y)^z
  -indicator_(x=y)N_(r,s)^z
  -N_(x,y)^r indicator_(z=s)
  -N_(x,y)^s indicator_(z=r).                         (P23.11z5zt)
```

The common five-fold invariant term cancels from the oriented difference.
The symmetric fusion identity

```text
N_(p,q)^a=N_(q,a)^p
```

cancels the common `r=s` term.  Condition `(P23.11z5zn)` then turns the
three cyclic instances of `(P23.11z5zt)` into `(P23.11z5zq)`.

It remains to prove that the displayed `C_F` does not exceed `R_F`.
Use the exact path decomposition

```text
I(p,q,a,r,s)
 =sum_(t in q star_k a) sum_(u in r star_k s) N_(t,u)^p. (P23.11z5zu)
```

If `r!=s`, each nonzero term of `C_F` supplies a path in
`(P23.11z5zu)` with respectively

```text
t=0,                  t=r,                  t=s.
```

These channels are distinct.  For the second and third choices, existence
follows from the full symmetry
`N_(r,s)^u=N_(r,u)^s=N_(s,u)^r`; choose any
`u in r star_k s`.  Hence `I>=C_F`.

Suppose `r=s`.  The first contribution still uses the distinct channel
`t=0`.  If `p=r` and `N_(q,a)^r=1`, the last two contributions require
two paths over `t=r`.  There are

```text
|r star_k r|=min(r,k-r)+1
```

such paths at finite level, and `r+1` ordinarily.  This is at least two
unless `r=k`.  In the exceptional simple-current case `r=p=k`, the
five-fold invariant supplies one path, while fusion symmetry gives

```text
N_(p,a)^q=N_(q,a)^p=1;
```

the extra term in `R_F` supplies the second unit.  Thus `C_F<=R_F` in all
cases.  Since `C_F>=0`, equality in the absolute-value inequality is
exactly equality of the two explicit quantities in
`(P23.11z5zq)`.  QED.

The first layer not covered by the six-factor theorem has an exact
ten-cut form.  It removes all singleton and redundant colour assignments
before any straightening argument.  For an indexed set
`P={p_1,...,p_5}`, write `m(L)` for the invariant multiplicity of the
listed tensor product, ordinary or in `SU(2)_k`.  For `E subset P`, the
notation remembers the indices even when labels coincide.

**Proposition 23A9S (equivalent five-plus `3|4` inequality).**  Assume

```text
q,a notin {p_1,...,p_5}.                              (P23.11z5zv)
```

Define

```text
N=m(q,a,P),

P_eq=indicator_(q=a)m(P)
     +sum_(E subset P, |E|=2)
        indicator_(the two labels in E are equal)m(q,a,P minus E),

U=sum_(i=1)^5 m(q,a,p_i)m(P minus {p_i})
  +sum_(E subset P, |E|=2)m(q,a,E)m(P minus E),

T=sum_(E subset P, |E|=2){
     m(q,E)m(a,P minus E)+m(a,E)m(q,P minus E)}.      (P23.11z5zw)
```

Then the signed contraction with minus labels `q,a` and the five plus
labels is exactly

```text
(1/2) tau_k tensor tau_k{
 d_q d_a product_(i=1)^5 S_(p_i)}
 =N+P_eq+U-T.                                         (P23.11z5zx)
```

Consequently the complete seven-factor, two-minus layer is equivalent to
the single indexed inequality

```text
T<=N+P_eq+U.                                          (P23.11z5zy)
```

This equivalence is uniform at the affine wall.  If one plus leaf `p_i`
is designated as the last fused update and `F` is the table of the other
four leaves, the left side of `(P23.11z5zx)` is
`B_(F^[p_i])(q,a)=R_F(p_i,q,a)+C_F(p_i,q;a)`.
Thus `(P23.11z5zy)` is also the explicit `3|4` form of the first
unresolved fused-triangle orientation.

**Proof.**  Expand the five plus factors by an indexed subset `A subset P`
assigned to the first coordinate, and pair each complete colour assignment
with its coordinate reversal.  Retain the representative in which the
minus label `q` lies in the first coordinate.  If `a` lies there as well,
the contribution is

```text
m(q,a,A)m(P minus A);
```

if `a` lies in the second coordinate, it is

```text
-m(q,A)m(a,P minus A).                                (P23.11z5zz)
```

Their sum over all `A` is the left side of `(P23.11z5zx)`.

For the negative term, `|A|=0` or `5` leaves a nontrivial singleton
component.  If `|A|=1`, its first component is the pair `{q,p_i}`; if
`|A|=4`, its second component is `{a,p_i}`.  All four cases vanish by
`(P23.11z5zv)`.  The surviving sizes `2` and `3` pair by complementation
and give exactly `T`.

For the positive term, `|A|=4` leaves a nontrivial singleton and vanishes.
The cases `|A|=5` and `0` give respectively `N` and the first term of
`P_eq`.  Size one gives the first sum in `U`, and size two gives its second
sum.  At size three, the complementary two-label invariant is nonzero
exactly when its two labels are equal; these are precisely the remaining
terms of `P_eq`.  This proves `(P23.11z5zx)` and hence the equivalence with
`(P23.11z5zy)`.

Finally, the boundary definition identifies

```text
B_(F^[p_i])(q,a)
 =sum_(t in q star_k a)F^[p_i](0,t)-F^[p_i](q,a)
```

with the same retained-coordinate assignment sum `(P23.11z5zz)`.
Lemma 23A9L gives its fused `R_F+C_F` form.  No stability assumption enters
either calculation.  QED.

The same inequality has a symmetric allocation-correlation form for
arbitrary minus labels.  This is the nonfundamental analogue of the
specialized identity in Lemma 23A9U3.

**Lemma 23A9S1 (exact arbitrary-minus allocation form).**  Under the
hypotheses of Proposition 23A9S, for an indexed subset of plus positions
`E subset [5]` write

```text
m_r(E)=multiplicity of V_r in product_(i in E)V_(p_i),
c_P(r,s)=sum_(E subset [5])m_r(E)m_s(E^c).
```

Then

```text
T=c_P(q,a),
N+P_eq+U=sum_(t in q star_k a)c_P(t,0).             (P23.11z5zy1)
```

Consequently the complete finite seven-factor inequality is exactly

```text
c_P(q,a)<=sum_(t in q star_k a)c_P(t,0).            (P23.11z5zy2)
```

If the maximal active-cut rank is at most two, every nonzero summand on
the left of `(P23.11z5zy2)` is one or two.

**Proof.**  In `c_P(q,a)`, subset sizes zero and five vanish because
`q,a>0`.  Sizes one and four vanish by the disjoint-support hypothesis.
The size-two terms are

```text
m_q(E)m_a(E^c),
```

the first orientation in `T`.  Complementing each size-three subset
gives the second orientation.  Hence `T=c_P(q,a)`.

For the positive side, associativity and multiplicity-free binary fusion
give

```text
sum_(t in q star_k a)c_P(t,0)
 =sum_(E subset [5])m(q,a,E)m_0(E^c).              (P23.11z5zy3)
```

The size-five term is `N`.  The size-zero term is
`indicator_(q=a)m(P)`.  Sizes one and two are respectively the two sums
defining `U`.  At size three, the complementary two-label invariant is
nonzero exactly when those two indexed plus labels are equal; these are
the equal-plus terms in `P_eq`.  Size four vanishes because its complement
is a nontrivial singleton.  This proves both identities in
`(P23.11z5zy1)` and hence `(P23.11z5zy2)`.

Finally a two-label block has multiplicity at most one.  Thus every
active term in `c_P(q,a)` is its complementary four-label rank, which is
one or two in the stated chamber.  QED.

Fusion depth at least three forces a sharp endpoint dichotomy on every
rank-one or rank-two complement.

**Lemma 23A9S2 (small-overlap endpoint skeleton).**  Assume the
hypotheses of Lemma 23A9S1 and

```text
|q star_k a|>=3.
```

Choose an active oriented cut with invariant triple
